seoTitle: South Granville vs South Gundurimba — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between South Granville and South Gundurimba. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: South Granville vs South Gundurimba: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Granville and South Gundurimba.
heading: South Granville vs South Gundurimba — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Granville, New South Wales and South Gundurimba, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Gundurimba has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to South Granville (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Granville or South Gundurimba safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Granville has a flood risk score of 43/100, while South Gundurimba scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Granville scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. South Gundurimba scores 38/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Granville or South Gundurimba?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Gundurimba has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
